Ensure coordination with other implementing departments such as in the case of Motorcycle Loans, the IPHO to have proper and regular coordination with municipal health offices within the province to check if outstanding loans of the exiting health personnel are settled before clearance is given. 4) The Provincial Accountant to check if adjustments should be made with regards to its settlement or only relevant disclosure is necessary. Prepare comprehensive monitoring of the Due to LGUs account and corresponding Loans Receivable - Others for proper check and balance of these accounts. 5) The Provincial Accountant to check if presenting the Loans Receivable - Others and related Due to LGUs account as current in the financial statements is correct and justifiable in compliance with the provisions stipulated in IPSAS 1, Presentation of Financial Statements, otherwise, perform proper adjustment in the books to comply with the aforesaid standard. 6) The Provincial Governor to direct the implementing department/s to fully utilize unused funds so that the benefits expected therefrom may already be enjoyed by the intended beneficiaries and avoid accumulation of collections in the Trust Fund while providing funds of the same purpose in the General Fund. Implementing departments to ensure compliance with sufficient documentation to establish validity and legitimacy of claims against the beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: 1) The Provincial Accounting Office (PACCO) and the Provincial Treasurer’s Office (PTO) to reconcile the balances of all accountable forms inventories reflected in their respective records. In relation to the discrepancies noted, the PTO to perform immediate disposal of obsolete accountable forms and the PACCO to record adjustments pertaining to unissued checks to CRAAF at year-end. 2) The Provincial Treasurer to comply with the procedures and controls on the requisition and issuance of accountable forms, particularly issuance of checkbooks to designated disbursing officers. In order to ensure proper monitoring of the movement of accountable forms, the designated custodian shall maintain a logbook which contains complete records of orders based on approved Requisition and Issue Slips. 3) In coordination with the PTO, the PACCO to perform thorough investigation on the factual status of Non-Accountable Forms Inventory amounting to ₱46,500.00 whether such has been issued already. If proven to be issued, the PACCO to record the necessary adjustment in the books. 4) The PTO to ensure complete presentment of all unused accountable forms during the conduct of physical count and the same is properly stored in the vault. In case of transfer of those unissued accountable forms outside its required location, the accountable officer to have proper documentation thereof for immediate reconciliation and verification of the resulting difference which may exist during physical count. 5) The Provincial Treasurer to ensure that all accountable forms are maintained under his custody and complete and timely reporting thereof is made on a monthly basis. The treasury department should see to it that all accountable forms are stored under its custody and complete accountability report is submitted to the accounting department and the auditor in a timely manner.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Assign staff to monitor and reconcile the balances of all accountable form inventories reflected in their respective records. The PTO to perform immediate disposal of obsolete accountable forms and the PACCO to record adjustments pertaining to unissued checks to CRAAF at year-end. The PTO to ensure complete presentment of all unused accountable forms during the conduct of physical count and the same is properly stored in the vault. observations.reason: Inadequate controls in the management of accountable forms and its non-reconciliation thereof in the accountability report and subsidiary ledger were noted. 1) The balances reported in the monthly consolidated report of accountability for accountable forms were not properly reconciled with the subsidiary ledger records at year-end. 2) The balances of Non-Accountable Forms Inventory amounting to ₱46,500.00 are non-moving as of December 31, 2020. 3) Inadequate controls as to ordering, storing and issuance of various accountable forms.

observations.recommendation: 1) The Provincial Development Council, with the Provincial Governor as Chairperson, to ensure that all PPAs funded in the annual and supplemental budgets are included in the approved investment program. The Provincial Planning and Development Office, being the secretariat of the PDC, must be cognizant of all new PPAs previously not included in the approved AIP and consider its inclusion in the supplemental investment program. 2) In coordination with the Provincial Accountant, the Local Finance Committee to determine the remaining balance of the BGP available considering the adjusted obligations and unrecorded payables. Thereafter, the Provincial Governor to direct the implementing departments to optimize the utilization of the remaining BGP pursuant to applicable budgeting rules and regulations and specific guidelines governing the grant. 3) The Local Finance Committee and the Provincial Accounting Department to adjust their respective reports on BGP to eliminate the noted discrepancies. Henceforth, perform monthly reconciliation of each balances pertaining to the grant in order to have effective monitoring and avoid unreliable reporting. 4) The Provincial Governor, to direct the implementing departments to execute without delay the related PPAs of COVID-19 so that timely and effective responses are achieved. Also review and analyze the necessity of unimplemented PPAs and consider its re-appropriation to other necessary COVID-19 response efforts. 5) The PDRRM Council to ensure that all changes in the investment program shall be supported by an approved council resolution. In case the changes in the original investment plan resulted to new PPAs, ensure inclusion thereof to the Supplemental Investment Program through proper and time coordination with the PDC. 6) The Provincial Governor, in coordination with implementing departments, to facilitate immediate implementation of programs and projects which was funded through donations. Ensure compliance with agreement made for the purpose and undertake immediate coordination with the donor in case the same was not satisfied prior to the receipt of the grant. 7) The Provincial Accounting Department to take up necessary adjustments pertaining to purchases of various supplies and equipment which were directly recorded as expenses and fixed assets despite the intended purpose of distributing the same to various public schools. Ensure completeness of issuance documents before crediting the related balances in the books. 8) The Provincial Governor, in coordination with the implementing departments, to execute without delay funded COVID-19-related PPAs in order to have timely and effective response to the threat. Also, review and analyze the necessity of unimplemented PPAs and consider its re-appropriation to other necessary COVID-19 response efforts.

The PGO through PGSO to implement strategies to compel concerned offices to submit Utilization/Consumption Reports for previous years and current releases to ensure proper accounting and reconciliation. The PAccO to take up necessary adjustments pertaining to purchases of various supplies and equipment which were directly recorded as expenses and fixed assets despite the intended purpose of distributing the same to various public schools. The Provincial Governor to enforce sanctions as provided in AO No. 09, series of 2015 to the implementing departments which failed to submit the required utilization reports.

observations.recommendation: 1) The PDRRM Council, headed by the Provincial Governor, to prepare LDRRMF Investment Plan for all available balances of DRRM Fund for the year, including transfers to Special Trust Fund. Regularly review and update such plans to consider current vulnerabilities and implement timely those approved pre-disaster PPAs. 2) The implementing departments headed by the Provincial Governor through the DRRM Officer, to follow the schedule of implementation of pre-disaster PPAs as embodied in the approved DRRM Investment Plans. In coordination with the Engineering Department, evaluate the causes of delays in the implementation of pre-disaster projects in order to provide immediate resolution therein and lessen or eliminate the vulnerabilities of the community in a timely manner. 3) The PDRRM Council through the DRRM Officer, in coordination with the Provincial Accounting Department, to properly identify the remaining balances of DRRM Fund as a result of fully completed PPAs and consider its re-appropriation to other priority DRRM PPAs which remained unfunded. Also, evaluate the status of those unimplemented PPAs if the same is still to be pursued, otherwise consider its realignment or reversion to other necessary DRRM PPAs. 4) The Provincial Planning and Development Office, in its quarterly reporting of Physical and Financial Accomplishment Report, to perform thorough validation of the physical and financial status of each program, projects and activities in order to ensure reconciliation thereof with the related reports of other concerned departments. 5) The Provincial Governor, to coordinate with the donor/funding agencies and implementing department/s in order to facilitate immediate implementation of programs and projects which was funded through donations. 6) The DRRM Officer, in coordination with other implementing departments, to reconcile the remaining balances of beginning DRRM inventories and cause the immediate submission of the related utilization reports and its supporting documents to the General Services Department for its subsequent submission to the Accounting Department for proper recording in the books. 7) The PDRRM Council through the DRRM Officer to periodically analyze the remaining balances of DRRM Fund and its related PPAs. 8) The Provincial Governor to enforce the sanctions as provided in AO No. 09, series of 2015 to the implementing departments which failed to submit the required utilization reports of various DRRM inventories held for distribution or consumption.

observations.recommendation: 1) The Provincial Governor to direct the implementing department/s to either draw up the appropriate project proposals to utilize fully idle or dormant trust funds or to refund excess project funds to the national treasury or request for transfer to the General Fund pursuant to the law governing said grant and/or the MOA/contract with the respective source agencies. 2) The Provincial Governor to direct the implementing departments to execute without delay the intended programs, projects, and activities of the fund transfers and other specific-purpose collections so that the benefits expected therefrom may already be enjoyed by the intended beneficiaries. 3) The PACCO, in reference to the information gathered from the implementing departments and source agencies as a result of the duly sent communication letters or demand letters, to determine the existence and validity of the balances reported. Determine and reconcile actual cash availability of those remaining/non-moving fund transfers through analysis of all cash in bank accounts in the Trust Fund. Once analyzed and proven to have available cash balance to back up the reported amounts, perform proper disposal thereof, otherwise, record necessary adjusting entries in the books to eliminate those unfunded programs and projects. 4) The PACCO, having performed reconciliation and recorded the necessary adjusting entries thereof but there are still remaining unadjusted balances, gather the required documents necessary to file the request for authority to write-off dormant fund transfers such as certification by the chief accountants and approved by the heads of the source and implementing agencies that the fund was utilized for the purpose, and certification from the recipient that the project was partially or fully implemented, supported by pictures of the implemented projects.

observations.recommendation: 1. The Provincial Governor to direct the implementing departments to prioritize the implementation of development projects and ensure that allocated funds are timely and optimally utilized in accordance with the guidelines prescribed under the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01. a) Implement an internal LFC policy through resolution or AO where offices which have not obligated their PPAs during the succeeding year’s AIP will not be accepted for funding under the 20% LDF in the succeeding year; b) Issuance of letter reminding the PEO and concerned offices regarding the PPAs which are not still implemented at the end of each quarter; c) Designate a staff under the PPDO who would regularly monitor the implementation of the PPAs; d) Implementation of “Open M&E” of Infrastructure Projects and “Open Contracting” as commitments under the Open Government Partnership (OGP); 2) The PPDO, in coordination with PEO, to develop specific strategies to effectively implement PPAs, both for current and continuing appropriations. Check unimplemented PPAs under 20% LDF, determine the causes of non-implementation and formulate relevant actions to address such. 3) The Provincial Development Council, with the PPDO as secretariat, to ensure inclusion of all funded local development projects in the approved annual or supplemental investment programs for a timely, effective and efficient implementation. 4) The Implementing Departments to ensure that program of works and other necessary procurement documents be prepared and submitted to PPDO at a reasonable time in order to hasten the implementation of PPAs during the planned schedule. Furthermore, The Implementing Departments to also check if there is a need to recalibrate its manpower accordingly. 5) The Implementing Departments to be responsive and prompt as to requests from PPDO regarding the progress of their projects in order to eventually improve the monitoring of PPAs. The PPDO to correspond proactively with regard to the results of monitoring.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Strengthen the monitoring of the implementation of the 20% LDF PPAs thru: a) Implement an internal LFC policy through resolution or AO where offices which have not obligated their PPAs during the succeeding year’s AIP will not be accepted for funding under the 20% LDF in the succeeding year; b) Issuance of letter reminding the PEO and concerned offices regarding the PPAs which are not still implemented at the end of each quarter; c) Designate a staff under the PPDO who would regularly monitor the implementation of the PPAs; d) Implementation of “Open M&E” of Infrastructure Projects and “Open Contracting” as commitments under the Open Government Partnership (OGP). The PEO will reset its target setting, reflect it in its OPCR, to accommodate for earlier project submission to prevent surge in 3rd quarter. PPDO and PEO will come up with project priority list so as to avoid delay in processing. PEO will effect organizational upgrade in its structure and logistics to support its new target setting. PEO will create Project Review Committee (PRC) to ensure prepared plans and POWs are complete and in order. PEO will recommend contractors who can undertake the projects with efficiency.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management strictly adhere to DILG-DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 particularly on the
inclusion of loan repayments in order not to sacrifice other priority programs and
projects. Moreover, fast track the implementation of the PPAs so that the ultimate
beneficiaries could enjoy the improved social, environment and economic condition
once the projects are completed. Furthermore, evaluate whether the unimplemented
projects from current and prior years’ appropriations are still implementable, or if
there is a need to revert funds to be used as new source of appropriations for more
priority development projects.

We recommended that the management:
1. Update the LDIP regularly to include new identified priority projects to
ensure that the implemented PPAs contributed to the achievement of
long-term goals; and ensure that only the PPAs culled from the Local
Development Investment Plan are included in the Annual Investment
Program and 20% Annual Development Plan; and
2. Require the City Development Council to identify and indicate the
specific starting month for the implementation of the prioritized projects
in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) for the guidance of the
implementing and monitoring office, thus ensuring that no delays will be
incurred for the implementation of the prioritized projects.

(Additional recommendations within Observation 2 - subparts)

For 2.2:
We recommended that, for annual planning and budgeting, and especially for
approval of payments, the City Accountant, City Treasurer, CPDC, City Budget
Office and all officials concerned determine with utmost care the loan
amortization amount based on the amortization schedule provided by the lending
bank.

For 2.3 (Audit of Purchase of 50 Hectares Lot(s) for Ecozone):
We recommended that, in order to avoid such issues as relocation of settlers
and be able to complete the PPAs as scheduled, the City Government conduct a
thorough study/investigation of projects, survey of project sites as pre-requisite to
their inclusion in the Annual Plan and Budget.

For 2.4 (Construction of Two-Storey Command Center):
We recommended the following:
1. Closely monitor the utilization and implementation of priority projects
for the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and
environmental management outcomes and to optimize fund utilization;
and
2. Fast track the implementation based on the scheduled timeframes
indicated in the AIP by the implementing department concerned to
achieve the desired management outcomes.

For 2.5 (Construction/Installation of Bio Medical and Solid Waste Facility
and Procurement of Lot for Bio Medical and Solid Waste Facility):
We recommended that the CPDO and CENRO:
1. Ensure that the proposed projects be carefully planned, assessed and
monitored in order to accomplish targets at the right time and purpose;
2. See to it that the projects identified in the ADP are feasible and can be
implemented within the time schedule to optimally utilize the 20%
Development Fund; and
3. Adopt alternative measures and strategies to ensure the viability and
effective implementation of the projects.

We recommended that –
1. management create a task force of competent professionals to completely
work back on the past year’s records and accounts, establishing cut-off for
current transactions, in order to determine the actual variances of PPE
account balances, and make a report thereon for appropriate definitive
action by higher authorities; and
2. thenceforth observe strictly the following control procedures, viz:

a) the City Accounting Office and the General Services Office conduct
periodic reconciliation of accounting and property records to come up
with correct information for PPE;

b) the City Accounting and the Property Officer come up with a similar and
uniform classification and recording of PPEs;

c) the General Service Officer shall coordinate with the City Accountant for
proper classification of the unserviceable items; and

d) the concerned supervisors shall endeavor to enhance improvement on
work competencies and technical skills, such as recommending the
Property Officer and other staff to attend seminar/on-the-job training on
property management.

Therefore, we recommended for the CDRRM Officer to closely monitor the
utilization and implementation of PPAs embodied in the annual LDRRM Plan in order
to achieve the desired outcome.

Henceforth, we also recommended the following:
1. For the CDRRMO, in coordination with the Council, to review and update
the LDRRMFIP annually;
2. For the LDRRMC to prepare the LDRRMFIP strictly in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 by incorporating the
detailed plan for the unexpended balance of the 30% Quick Response Fund
(QRF) and 70% MOOE of the LDRRMF for proper utilization of the
LDRRMF; and
3. For the CDRRMO to submit written explanation for the reason/s on
disbursing/obligating the prior years’ unexpended balance without inclusion
in the revised LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC Secretariat to faithfully
comply with the posting requirements of GPPB Circular 01-2020, guided by the GPPB-
TSO Advisory 04-202010 and 05-2020.11

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct Department Heads to exert effort for the possible collection of the long overdue accounts totaling P889,793.25 to generate additional funds for the City’s operation. Send demand letters directly to concerned persons requiring them to settle their accounts, otherwise enforce settlement thru deductions from salaries or other compensation due the persons liable.
observations.managementAction: Refund of disallowance still on-going

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for management to require the City Treasurer and the City Accountant to remit on time the exact amounts deducted and withheld from employees’ salaries to GSIS, Pag-ibig, Philhealth, and BIR, in  accordance with  Sections 6(b) and 52 (g) of RA 8291 or  the GSIS Act of 1997, Sec. 22 (a) of RA 7742 which governs the HDMF or Pag-ibig Fund, Section 20(b) and 44 of RA 7875 or the National Internal Revenue Code of 1997, and DOF-DBM-COA Joint Circular No. 1-2000 as amended by Circular No.1-2000A, to avoid possible penalties and the adverse effect on the claims and benefits accruing to the employees.

We also recommended instructing the City Accountant to trace and identify the prior years’ unaccounted variances on GSIS, HDMF, PHIC and BIR to ensure the accuracy/reliability of the reported balance of the Trust Liabilities accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation still on-going

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City General Services Office (CGSO) ensure that all motor vehicles owned by the city government are marked “For Official Use Only” to show the public that utilization of government resources are in order and in accordance with rules and regulations. Once marked “For Official Use Only”, government officials and employees would have second thought of using government vehicle or probably dissuade them from utilizing the same for purposes other than official functions.
observations.managementAction: Implementation was done phase by phase.

observations.recommendation:
Require the implementing offices to exert diligent efforts to implement the identified development projects in line with the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011, to ensure that the intended beneficiaries are not deprived of the benefits that they should have enjoyed.


Likewise, enjoin offices concerned to evaluate whether the unimplemented projects from current and prior years’ appropriations are still implementable, or that there is a need to revert funds to be used as new source of appropriations for more priority development projects.

And, advice City Officials involved in the formulation, development and implementation of programs/projects to faithfully discharge their functions and responsibilities as provided in the Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: Some projects were already fully implemented while others are yet to be implemented.

observations.recommendation: Provide the needed equipment required for the full operation of the Halal Slaughterhouse so that the City Government could generate additional income from said operation.

We recommended that management avail of the services provided by the LBP through an Automated Teller Machine (ATM) for payment of salaries to its permanent employees as well as casual and job order personnel in order to promote operational efficiency as well as strengthened internal control on cash and to avoid further the risk of possible loss/ misapplication.
observations.managementAction: MOA is already signed between the City Government and LBP for the services.
